title: DetachedFromVisualTreeTrigger not triggered in v12.0.5
author: shine1258
body:
In v12.0.0.1, the console would output "Detached" when switching tabs. After upgrading to v12.0.5, there is no output, and breakpoint is not hit.
```xaml
  <TabControl>
    <TabItem Header="Tab 1">
      <TextBlock Text="Tab 1">
        <Interaction.Behaviors>
          <DetachedFromVisualTreeTrigger>
            <InvokeCommandAction Command="{Binding OnDetached}" />
          </DetachedFromVisualTreeTrigger>
        </Interaction.Behaviors>
      </TextBlock>
    </TabItem>

    <TabItem Header="Tab 2">
      <TextBlock Text="Tab 2">
        <Interaction.Behaviors>
          <DetachedFromVisualTreeTrigger>
            <InvokeCommandAction Command="{Binding OnDetached}" />
          </DetachedFromVisualTreeTrigger>
        </Interaction.Behaviors>
      </TextBlock>
    </TabItem>
  </TabControl>
```
```C#
    public void OnDetached() => Console.WriteLine("Detached");
```

title: Backport workflow-hardening fix (excessive-permissions) to 5 release branches
author: CharlieMCY
body:
### Summary The default branch already hardened `.github/workflows/build.yml` against the issue(s) below, but **5** release branches still carry it. This proposes the same, minimal, scanner-verified fix for each. ### What's flagged (by [zizmor](https://github.com/woodruffw/zizmor)) - `excessive-permissions` — workflow/job granted broader `permissions` than needed Already resolved on the default branch in: - https://github.com/wieslawsoltes/Xaml.Behaviors/commit/c1912f789a54fdfab83f7e48dd14ee69bbfc184b ### Affected release branches (5) - **`release/11.3.0-beta2`** (still present as of HEAD `b57e7c57`) - **`release/11.2.7`** (still present as of HEAD `99fa7cee`) - **`release/11.2.6`** (still present as of HEAD `cf77469a`) - **`release/11.3.0-beta1`** (still present as of HEAD `cd5752ef`) - **`release/11.2.0.14`** (still present as of HEAD `0c303a69`) ### Suggested per-branch patches Each diff below was checked locally with **zizmor** and **actionlint**: the flagged finding(s) are cleared on the affected construct and no new lint or security findings are introduced. (Whitespace is normalized; only security-relevant lines change.)
release/11.3.0-beta2 — excessive-permissions File `.github/workflows/build.yml`; suggested edits: - + permissions.contents = 'read' - + permissions.pull-requests = 'write' ```diff --- a/.github/workflows/build.yml +++ b/.github/workflows/build.yml @@ -25,3 +25,6 @@ run: dotnet build --configuration Release - name: Test Release run: dotnet test --configuration Release +permissions: + contents: read + pull-requests: write ```
release/11.2.7 — excessive-permissions File `.github/workflows/build.yml`; suggested edits: - + permissions.contents = 'read' - + permissions.pull-requests = 'write' ```diff --- a/.github/workflows/build.yml +++ b/.github/workflows/build.yml @@ -25,3 +25,6 @@ run: dotnet build --configuration Release - name: Test Release run: dotnet test --configuration Release +permissions: + contents: read + pull-requests: write ```
release/11.2.6 — excessive-permissions File `.github/workflows/build.yml`; suggested edits: - + permissions.contents = 'read' - + permissions.pull-requests = 'write' ```diff --- a/.github/workflows/build.yml +++ b/.github/workflows/build.yml @@ -25,3 +25,6 @@ run: dotnet build --configuration Release - name: Test Release run: dotnet test --configuration Release +permissions: + contents: read + pull-requests: write ```
release/11.3.0-beta1 — excessive-permissions File `.github/workflows/build.yml`; suggested edits: - + permissions.contents = 'read' - + permissions.pull-requests = 'write' ```diff --- a/.github/workflows/build.yml +++ b/.github/workflows/build.yml @@ -25,3 +25,6 @@ run: dotnet build --configuration Release - name: Test Release run: dotnet test --configuration Release +permissions: + contents: read + pull-requests: write ```
release/11.2.0.14 — excessive-permissions File `.github/workflows/build.yml`; suggested edits: - + permissions.contents = 'read' - + permissions.pull-requests = 'write' ```diff --- a/.github/workflows/build.yml +++ b/.github/workflows/build.yml @@ -25,3 +25,6 @@ run: dotnet build --configuration Release - name: Test Release run: dotnet test --configuration Release +permissions: + contents: read + pull-requests: write ```
--- *Happy to open pull requests instead if that's preferred.*

title: Flyout Opened/Closed events not triggered with EventTriggerBehavior in Xaml.Behaviors v12
author: DarioKipaca

title: Feature request: opt-in RevertOnFalse for DataTriggerBehavior and MultiDataTriggerBehavior
author: DamirLisak
body:
Today DataTriggerBehavior only applies actions when the condition is true, but it does not automatically revert changes when the condition becomes false. This makes simple UI scenarios harder, for example toggling a property value back to its original state. (This automatic revert has been a standard feature in WPF when Style-Setters have been set)

**Proposal:**
Add a new boolean property RevertOnFalse (default false) to:
- DataTriggerBehavior
- MultiDataTriggerBehavior

**Behavior:**
- default false: keep current behavior exactly as it is today
- true: apply actions on enter, revert reversible actions on exit

**Why:**
- keeps backward compatibility
- gives a clean opt-in path for reversible trigger behavior
- removes the need for manual inverse trigger definitions in common cases

title: RoutedEventTriggerBase doesn’t seem to dispose correctly when removed from and re-added to the visual tree
author: moonwell0106
body:
test:

```
<UserControl  x:Class="AvaloniaApplication1.UserControl1">      
      <Border Width="300" Height="300" Background="Red">
         <Interaction.Behaviors>
             <PointerEnteredTrigger >
				 <LogAction Argument="in"></LogAction>
             </PointerEnteredTrigger>
        </Interaction.Behaviors>
        <TextBlock>Test</TextBlock>
    </Border>
</UserControl>

<Window>
    <StackPanel x:Name="sp">
        <local:UserControl1 x:Name="uc"></local:UserControl1>
		<Button Click="Button_Click">Addremove</Button>
    </StackPanel>
</Window>

        private void Button_Click(object? sender, Avalonia.Interactivity.RoutedEventArgs e)
        {
            sp.Children.Remove(uc);
            sp.Children.Add(uc);
        }
```

Each time the button is clicked, LogAction executes one additional time when PointerEnteredTrigger fires

title: PropertyHelper.FindAvaloniaAttachedProperty doesn't correctly searches for attached property
author: ExLuzZziVo
body: Hi, I've encountered a problem: I can't use ```ChangePropertyAction``` with attached properties. While investigating the issue, I discovered that ```PropertyHelper.FindAvaloniaAttachedProperty``` is incorrectly selecting the type in this line: ```var targetType = GetTypeByName(targetPropertyTypeName) ?? targetObject.GetType();```, because the ```AvaloniaPropertyRegistry.Instance.GetRegisteredAttached()``` method looks up the attached property by the _target object_ type(e.g. TextBox), not the attached property type.

title: Incorrect assembly resolution for Grid type (resolved to AvaloniaUI.DiagnosticsSupport instead of Avalonia.Controls)
author: sttong2025
body:
<Interaction.Behaviors>
	<DataTriggerBehavior Binding="{Binding (mah:ToggleButtonHelper.ContentDirection), RelativeSource={RelativeSource TemplatedParent}}"
						 Value="RightToLeft">
		<interactions:ChangePropertyActionEx TargetObject="ContentPresenter" PropertyName="Grid.Column" Value="0" />
		<ChangePropertyAction TargetObject="LeftCol" PropertyName="Width" Value="{x:Static GridLength.Star}" />
		<ChangePropertyAction TargetObject="Radio" PropertyName="Grid.Column" Value="1" />
		<ChangePropertyAction TargetObject="RightCol" PropertyName="Width" Value="{x:Static GridLength.Auto}" />
	</DataTriggerBehavior>
</Interaction.Behaviors>

  private static Type? GetTypeByName(string name)
 {
     Assembly[] assemblies = AppDomain.CurrentDomain.GetAssemblies();
     return (from assembly in assemblies.AsEnumerable().Reverse()
             select assembly.GetType(name)).FirstOrDefault((Type t) => (object)t != null) ?? assemblies.AsEnumerable().Reverse().SelectMany((Assembly assembly) => assembly.GetTypes())
         .FirstOrDefault((Type t) => t.Name == name);
 }
-------------------------------------------------------------
When I debug, I find the type is in AvaloniaUI.DiagnosticsSupport. It should be Avalonia.Controls

title: ToolTipClosingTrigger and ToolTipOpeningTrigger Error
author: pkq520
body:
I have tested both ToolTClosingTrigger and ToolOpeningTrigger using Avalonia and found that they cannot be triggered properly. Even if I use them:
```
<i:Interaction.Behaviors>
    <ia:EventTriggerBehavior EventName="ToolTipOpening">
        <ia:InvokeCommandAction Command="{Binding OpenCommand}" />
    </ia:EventTriggerBehavior>

    <ia:EventTriggerBehavior EventName="ToolTipClosed">
        <ia:InvokeCommandAction Command="{Binding CloseCommand}" />
    </ia:EventTriggerBehavior>
</i:Interaction.Behaviors>
```
Similarly, it cannot be triggered normally.
I also look https://wieslawsoltes.github.io/Xaml.Behaviors/articles/interactions-custom/tool-tips/tool-tip-opening-trigger.html 

AvaloniaVersion 11.3.8
Xaml.Behaviors.Avalonia 11.3.8.2

title: RoutedEventTriggerBehavior fails to trigger on window close
author: swety2003
body:
## Bug Description

RoutedEventTriggerBehavior fails to trigger actions when attached to a Window and the window is closed.

## Steps to Reproduce

1. Attach RoutedEventTriggerBehavior to a Window
2. Configure a RoutedEvent (e.g., Window.WindowClosedEvent) with an action (e.g., InvokeCommandAction)
3. Close the window
4. Observe that the action is never executed

## Root Cause

When a Window is closed, the following sequence occurs:

1. Window close
2. OnDetachedFromVisualTree() is called on the behavior
3. RemoveHandler() is invoked, removing the event handler
4. The WindowClosedEvent is then fired
5. Since the handler has already been removed, the event cannot be captured

This is a race condition between the visual tree detachment and the routed event firing.

## Additional Information

- Xaml.Behaviors version: 12.0.0
- Avalonia version: 12.0.0

## Reproduction Code
```xml
<Window 
    xmlns="https://github.com/avaloniaui" >
        <RoutedEventTriggerBehavior RoutedEvent="{x:Static Window.WindowClosedEvent}">
            <InvokeCommandAction Command="{Binding WindowClosedCommand}" />
        </RoutedEventTriggerBehavior>
</Window>

title: ContextDragBehavior from ListBox doesn't work when scrolling
author: PlechovaPlechovka
body:
Items cannot be dragged from ListBox when such item is not visible on the application window after application start.

Steps to reproduce:

1. Build and run attached project
2. Try to drag any visible item from the ListBox - it works ("Drop failed" cursor is shown)
3. Scroll a few items down. Try to drag any new visible item - no drag happens (no "Drop failed" cursor is shown)

[XamlBehaviorsDragAndDropTest.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/26412582/XamlBehaviorsDragAndDropTest.zip)

title: DataTriggerBehavior not work when property's value is Null
author: realMJZ
body:
``` xml
<TextBlock>
  <Interaction.Behaviors>
    <DataTriggerBehavior Binding="{Binding TestProp}" Value="{x:Null}">
      <ChangePropertyAction PropertyName="Text" Value="Empty"/>
    </DataTriggerBehavior>
  </Interaction.Behaviors>
</TextBlock>
```
``` csharp
// nothing happened
TestProp = null;
```

title: Make validation rules have bindable properties
author: ZzZombo
body: Right now you can't easily express in  XAML something like `<RangeValidationRule Minimum="{Binding Min}" Maximum="{Binding Max}" />`. Making rules bindable should only require them to inherit from `AvaloniaObject` and reimplement each property, including the message, as `StyledProperty`, which I had done in my project where I needed that.

title: EventTriggerBehavior not firing under AOT
author: PanDisheng
body: This EventTriggerBehavior fails to be triggered in AOT mode, while it works properly in non-AOT mode. It seems to be an issue with the behavior itself, whereas data-related behaviors can be triggered normally.

title: Request: Avalonia 12 preview version
author: Deadpikle
body:
Howdy,

This is obviously no rush at all -- Avalonia 12 is only on preview 1 after all. But if you have time and are able, could you put out a preview of this lib that uses Avalonia 12? That would allow those of us who also want to try out the Avalonia 12 to do so. 😁 

Thanks much!

title: XmlnsDefinition not found
author: lloydjatkinson
body:
Hi, when I use behaviors the preview displays an error about syntax. However, the build succeeds and the app runs fine.

I am using.NET 10, Avalonia 11.3.10, and Xaml.Behaviors 11.3.9.

The following code triggers the following stack trace.
```xaml
    <Interaction.Behaviors>
    </Interaction.Behaviors>
```

I checked the docs (https://wieslawsoltes.github.io/Xaml.Behaviors/articles/interactivity/xmlns-definitions.html) and it should be applying them automatically?

I am already declaring some XML namespaces in my .csproj, is it possible that is causing this?

```xml
<ItemGroup>
  <AssemblyAttribute Include="Avalonia.Metadata.XmlnsDefinitionAttribute">
    <_Parameter1>https://mazes/features</_Parameter1>
    <_Parameter2>Mazes.UI.Features.ThemeSelect</_Parameter2>
  </AssemblyAttribute>

</ItemGroup>

title: EditableItem.axaml : DoubleTappedEvent not working
author: RomaNNBk
body:
In version 11.3.0.6, ShowOnDoubleTappedBehavior was used, and everything worked fine.
In version 11.3.0.7, ShowOnDoubleTappedBehavior was replaced with InlineEditBehavior, where it didn't work.

InlineEditBehavior uses the DoubleTappedEvent with RoutingStrategies.Tunnel. If I change RoutingStrategies to Bubble (ShowOnDoubleTappedBehavior used RoutingStrategies.Bubble), the event starts working, but I'm not sure how correct is it.

title: The InvokeButtonClickAction fails to trigger the button.
author: yjkt
body:
```csharp
<StackPanel Background="Pink">
    <Button Name="Btn11" Content="Click Me" Command="{Binding BtnClick}"/>


    <Border Margin="10" Name="MyBorder" Background="red">
        <Interaction.Behaviors>
			<EventTriggerBehavior EventName="PointerPressed">
                <!--<InvokeCommandAction Command="{Binding BtnClick}"/>-->
                <InvokeButtonClickAction TargetButton="{Binding #Btn11}"/>
            </EventTriggerBehavior>
        </Interaction.Behaviors>
        <Label Content="{Binding Greeting}" Background="Purple" HorizontalAlignment="Left"></Label>
    </Border>
	
</StackPanel>
```

Avalonia 11.3.9
Windows 11 x64

`<InvokeCommandAction Command="{Binding BtnClick}"/>` works properly, but `<InvokeButtonClickAction TargetButton="{Binding #Btn11}"/>` is not work.
